protocols.name: Chest Pain / ACS
assessmentItems: Blood pressure both arms if dissection suspected
suggestedActions: Consider 12-lead ECG if available per protocol.
Consider aspirin 324 mg PO if no contraindications — verify per local protocol.
Consider IV access and normal saline TKO.
Consider nitroglycerin if SBP > 100 — verify per local protocol and medical direction.
Monitor SpO2; consider O2 if < 94%.
Obtain OPQRST and SAMPLE history.
redFlags: SBP < 90 or signs of cardiogenic shock — contact medical direction immediately.
New ST elevation on 12-lead — consider STEMI alert per regional protocol.
Tearing pain radiating to back — consider aortic dissection, avoid anticoagulants.
medicationNotes: Aspirin 324 mg PO — verify no allergy, no active GI bleed.
Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL — verify SBP > 100, no recent PDE5 inhibitor use. Contact medical direction if required.
Consider fentanyl for pain management — verify per local protocol and medical direction.
protocols.disclaimer: Advisory guidance only. Verify all actions per local chest pain/ACS protocol. Contact medical direction as required.

protocols.name: Respiratory Distress
assessmentItems: Ability to speak in full sentences
suggestedActions: Consider O2 to maintain SpO2 > 94% — verify per local protocol.
Consider nebulized albuterol for bronchospasm — verify per local protocol.
Consider CPAP if available and indicated — verify per local protocol.
Position of comfort; consider sitting upright.
Establish IV access if condition is severe.
Consider epinephrine if anaphylaxis suspected — verify per local protocol.
redFlags: SpO2 < 90% despite O2 — consider advanced airway, contact medical direction.
Silent chest with severe distress — impending respiratory failure.
Altered mental status with respiratory distress — immediate intervention needed.
medicationNotes: Albuterol 2.5 mg nebulized — verify per local protocol.
Epinephrine 0.3 mg IM for anaphylaxis only — verify per local protocol.
pcrDocumentationNeeds: Lung sounds before and after treatment
protocols.disclaimer: Advisory guidance only. Verify all actions per local respiratory distress protocol. Contact medical direction as required.

protocols.name: Altered Mental Status
assessmentItems: Medication bottles or paraphernalia on scene
suggestedActions: Consider blood glucose check — treat hypoglycemia per local protocol.
Consider naloxone if opioid overdose suspected — verify per local protocol.
Protect airway; consider recovery position if no trauma suspected.
Consider stroke scale (Cincinnati or similar) — verify per local protocol.
Note last known normal time for potential stroke.
Establish IV access.
redFlags: GCS ≤ 8 — consider advanced airway management, contact medical direction.
Blood glucose < 60 with AMS — administer dextrose per protocol.
Unilateral findings (facial droop, arm drift) — activate stroke alert per regional protocol.
Pinpoint pupils with respiratory depression — consider naloxone.
medicationNotes: Dextrose D10 or D50 for confirmed hypoglycemia — verify per local protocol.
Naloxone 2 mg IN or 0.4 mg IV — titrate to respiratory effort, not full alertness. Verify per local protocol.
protocols.disclaimer: Advisory guidance only. Verify all actions per local altered mental status protocol. Contact medical direction as required.

protocols.name: Stroke / CVA
suggestedActions: Consider stroke scale assessment (Cincinnati or similar) — verify per local protocol.
Document last known normal time precisely.
Consider stroke alert activation per regional protocol.
Consider blood glucose check — rule out hypoglycemia.
Consider transport to nearest stroke center — verify per local protocol.
Do not lower blood pressure unless directed by medical control.
redFlags: Last known normal < 4.5 hours — time-critical, expedite transport to stroke center.
Rapidly declining GCS — consider advanced airway, contact medical direction.
Seizure activity — manage airway and protect patient, contact medical direction.
SBP > 220 — contact medical direction before treating.
medicationNotes: No anticoagulants or antiplatelets unless directed by medical control.
Blood pressure management only per medical direction.
medicalDirectionRequired: Destination decision if multiple stroke centers available
pcrDocumentationNeeds: Stroke scale with each component documented
Exact last known normal time and source
protocols.disclaimer: Advisory guidance only. Verify all actions per local stroke protocol. Contact medical direction as required. Time-critical condition — minimize scene time.

protocols.name: Trauma / Injury
suggestedActions: Consider primary survey: ABCDE — address life threats first.
Consider direct pressure for hemorrhage control; consider tourniquet for extremity if needed — verify per local protocol.
Consider spinal motion restriction if mechanism suggests — verify per local protocol.
Consider IV access with normal saline; titrate to SBP > 90.
Consider rapid transport if trauma center criteria met — verify per local protocol.
Expose and examine — check for hidden injuries.
redFlags: Uncontrolled hemorrhage — apply tourniquet, contact medical direction.
Signs of tension pneumothorax (absent breath sounds, JVD, hypotension) — consider needle decompression per protocol.
GCS < 9 with trauma — manage airway, consider trauma center.
Pelvic instability — apply binder, limit movement, expedite transport.
medicationNotes: Normal saline — titrate to SBP > 90, avoid over-resuscitation. Verify per local protocol.
Consider TXA for significant hemorrhage within 3 hours of injury — verify per local protocol and medical direction.
pcrDocumentationNeeds: All hemorrhage control measures with times
protocols.disclaimer: Advisory guidance only. Verify all actions per local trauma protocol. Contact medical direction as required. Consider trauma center criteria.
